PEG 0.80 (cheapest in the group) with forward P/E 22.2 vs trailing 38.3 — earnings growth outpacing the multiple.
Target_mean_price $1528.55 implies ~26.7% upside; median $1521.50 ~26.1%.
Negative book_value (-90.219) and price_to_book (-13.4) reflect aggressive buybacks (equity bought back below par), not distress — 95.8% institutional ownership.
Elevated and rising short interest (10.49%, +13.85%) with a 6.1-day cover ratio is the key risk/overhang flag.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Re-test toward the sma zone / prior base if mortgage-origination volumes stay soft or a short thesis gains traction.

comparables.note: S&P Global — analytics/ratings peer; FICO grows faster (39%) at a lower forward multiple and PEG.

growth_picks.analyst_take: A wide-moat, 84%-margin scores franchise growing revenue 39% and earnings 69%, now 40% off its high at a PEG of 0.80 — the best growth-at-a-price setup in the group. Two flags temper it: ROE is uncomputable on buyback-driven negative equity (scored as insufficient_data), and short interest is high (10.5%) and rising, so expect volatility. Quality and valuation justify a BUY, ideally accumulated on weakness toward the 50-day.
